\"borderBottomLeftRadius\", \"borderBottomRightRadius\",\n    \"fontSize\",\n    \"letterSpacing\", \"wordSpacing\", \"unityParagraphSpacing\",\n])\n\n// Style properties that expect color values\nconst COLOR_PROPERTIES = new Set([\n    \"color\", \"backgroundColor\",\n    \"borderColor\", \"borderTopColor\", \"borderRightColor\", \"borderBottomColor\", \"borderLeftColor\",\n    \"unityTextOutlineColor\", \"unityBackgroundImageTintColor\",\n])\n\n// Style properties that are plain numbers (no Length wrapper needed)\nconst NUMBER_PROPERTIES = new Set([\n    \"flexGrow\", \"flexShrink\", \"opacity\",\n    \"unitySliceTop\", \"unitySliceRight\", \"unitySliceBottom\", \"unitySliceLeft\", \"unitySliceScale\",\n    \"borderWidth\", \"borderTopWidth\", \"borderRightWidth\", \"borderBottomWidth\", \"borderLeftWidth\",\n    \"unityTextOutlineWidth\",\n])\n\n// Enum property mappings: React style value -> Unity enum value\n// Keys are camelCase (React/CSS style), values map to Unity enum member names\nconst ENUM_MAPPINGS: Record<string, { enum: () => Record<string, number>, values: Record<string, string> }> = {\n    flexDirection: {\n        enum: () => CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.FlexDirection,\n        values: {\n            \"row\": \"Row\",\n            \"row-reverse\": \"RowReverse\",\n            \"column\": \"Column\",\n            \"column-reverse\": \"ColumnReverse\",\n        }\n    },\n    flexWrap: {\n        enum: () => CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.Wrap,\n        values: {\n            \"nowrap\": \"NoWrap\",\n            \"wrap\": \"Wrap\",\n            \"wrap-reverse\": \"WrapReverse\",\n        }\n    },\n    alignItems: {\n        enum: () => CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.Align,\n        values: {\n            \"auto\": \"Auto\",\n            \"flex-start\": \"FlexStart\",\n            \"flex-end\": \"FlexEnd\",\n            \"center\": \"Center\",\n            \"stretch\": \"Stretch\",\n        }\n    },\n    alignSelf: {\n        enum: () => CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.Align,\n        values: {\n            \"auto\": \"Auto\",\n            \"flex-start\": \"FlexStart\",\n            \"flex-end\": \"FlexEnd\",\n            \"center\": \"Center\",\n            \"stretch\": \"Stretch\",\n        }\n    },\n    alignContent: {\n        enum: () => CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.Align,\n        values: {\n            \"auto\": \"Auto\",\n            \"flex-start\": \"FlexStart\",\n            \"flex-end\": \"FlexEnd\",\n            \"center\": \"Center\",\n            \"stretch\": \"Stretch\",\n        }\n    },\n    justifyContent: {\n        enum: () => CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.Justify,\n        values: {\n            \"flex-start\": \"FlexStart\",\n            \"flex-end\": \"FlexEnd\",\n            \"center\": \"Center\",\n            \"space-between\": \"SpaceBetween\",\n            \"space-around\": \"SpaceAround\",\n        }\n    },\n    position: {\n        enum: () => CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.Position,\n        values: {\n            \"relative\": \"Relative\",\n            \"absolute\": \"Absolute\",\n        }\n    },\n    overflow: {\n        enum: () => CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.Overflow,\n        values: {\n            \"visible\": \"Visible\",\n            \"hidden\": \"Hidden\",\n        }\n    },\n    display: {\n        enum: () => CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.DisplayStyle,\n        values: {\n            \"flex\": \"Flex\",\n            \"none\": \"None\",\n        }\n    },\n    visibility: {\n        enum: () => CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.Visibility,\n        values: {\n            \"visible\": \"Visible\",\n            \"hidden\": \"Hidden\",\n        }\n    },\n    whiteSpace: {\n        enum: () => CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.WhiteSpace,\n        values: {\n            \"normal\": \"Normal\",\n            \"nowrap\": \"NoWrap\",\n        }\n    },\n    unityFontStyleAndWeight: {\n        enum: () => CS.UnityEngine.FontStyle,\n        values: {\n            \"normal\": \"Normal\",\n            \"bold\": \"Bold\",\n            \"italic\": \"Italic\",\n            \"bold-and-italic\": \"BoldAndItalic\",\n        }\n    },\n    textOverflow: {\n        enum: () => CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.TextOverflow,\n        values: {\n            \"clip\": \"Clip\",\n            \"ellipsis\": \"Ellipsis\",\n        }\n    },\n    unityTextOverflowPosition: {\n        enum: () => CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.TextOverflowPosition,\n        values: {\n            \"end\": \"End\",\n            \"start\": \"Start\",\n            \"middle\": \"Middle\",\n        }\n    },\n    unityOverflowClipBox: {\n        enum: () => CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.OverflowClipBox,\n        values: {\n            \"padding-box\": \"PaddingBox\",\n            \"content-box\": \"ContentBox\",\n        }\n    },\n}\n\n/**\n * Parse an enum style value\n * @param key - Style property name\n * @param value - String value from React style (e.g., \"row\", \"flex-start\")\n * @returns Unity enum value or null if not found\n */\nfunction parseEnumValue(key: string, value: string): number | null {\n    const mapping = ENUM_MAPPINGS[key]\n    if (!mapping) return null\n\n    const unityEnumName = mapping.values[value]\n    if (!unityEnumName) return null\n\n    const enumType = mapping.enum()\n    return enumType[unityEnumName] ?? null\n}\n\n/**\n * Parse a length value from various formats\n * @param value - number, \"100\", \"100px\", \"50%\", \"auto\"\n * @returns Unity Length struct or StyleKeyword\n */\nexport function parseLength(value: number | string): CSLength | number | null {\n    if (typeof value === \"number\") {\n        return new CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.Length(value, CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.LengthUnit.Pixel)\n    }\n\n    if (typeof value !== \"string\") return null\n\n    const trimmed = value.trim().toLowerCase()\n\n    // Handle keywords\n    if (trimmed === \"auto\") {\n        return CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.StyleKeyword.Auto\n    }\n    if (trimmed === \"none\") {\n        return CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.StyleKeyword.None\n    }\n    if (trimmed === \"initial\") {\n        return CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.StyleKeyword.Initial\n    }\n\n    // Parse numeric values with units\n    const match = trimmed.match(/^(-?[\\d.]+)(px|%)?$/)\n    if (match) {\n        const num = parseFloat(match[1])\n        if (isNaN(num)) return null\n\n        const unitStr = match[2]\n        const unit = unitStr === \"%\"\n            ? CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.LengthUnit.Percent\n            : CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.LengthUnit.Pixel\n\n        return new CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.Length(num, unit)\n    }\n\n    return null\n}\n\n/**\n * Clamp a number to [0, 1] range\n */\nfunction clamp01(n: number): number {\n    return n < 0 ? 0 : n > 1 ? 1 : n\n}\n\n/**\n * Create a Unity Color with clamped values\n */\nfunction createColor(r: number, g: number, b: number, a: number): CSColor {\n    return new CS.UnityEngine.Color(clamp01(r), clamp01(g), clamp01(b), clamp01(a))\n}\n\n/**\n * Parse hex color string into RGBA components\n * Supports: #rgb, #rgba, #rrggbb, #rrggbbaa\n */\nfunction parseHexColor(hex: string): CSColor | null {\n    const len = hex.length\n    const isShort = len === 3 || len === 4\n    const isLong = len === 6 || len === 8\n    if (!isShort && !isLong) return null\n\n    const step = isShort ? 1 : 2\n    const parse = (i: number): number => {\n        const slice = hex.slice(i * step, i * step + step)\n        const expanded = isShort ? slice + slice : slice\n        return parseInt(expanded, 16) / 255\n    }\n\n    const r = parse(0), g = parse(1), b = parse(2)\n    const a = (len === 4 || len === 8) ? parse(3) : 1\n    return createColor(r, g, b, a)\n}\n\n/**\n * Parse a color value from various formats\n * @param value - \"#fff\", \"#ffffff\", \"#ffffffff\", \"rgb(255,0,0)\", \"rgba(255,0,0,0.5)\", \"red\"\n * @returns Unity Color struct or null if invalid\n */\nexport function parseColor(value: string): CSColor | null {\n    if (typeof value !== \"string\") return null\n\n    const trimmed = value.trim().toLowerCase()\n\n    // Named colors\n    if (NAMED_COLORS[trimmed]) {\n        const [r, g, b, a] = NAMED_COLORS[trimmed]\n        return new CS.UnityEngine.Color(r, g, b, a)\n    }\n\n    // Hex colors\n    if (trimmed.startsWith(\"#\")) {\n        return parseHexColor(trimmed.slice(1))\n    }\n\n    // rgb(r, g, b) or rgba(r, g, b, a) - supports both integer and percentage values\n    const rgbMatch = trimmed.match(/^rgba?\\s*\\(\\s*([\\d.]+)(%?)\\s*,\\s*([\\d.]+)(%?)\\s*,\\s*([\\d.]+)(%?)\\s*(?:,\\s*([\\d.]+))?\\s*\\)$/)\n    if (rgbMatch) {\n        const isPercent = rgbMatch[2] === \"%\"\n        const divisor = isPercent ? 100 : 255\n        const r = parseFloat(rgbMatch[1]) / divisor\n        const g = parseFloat(rgbMatch[3]) / divisor\n        const b = parseFloat(rgbMatch[5]) / divisor\n        const a = rgbMatch[7] !== undefined ? parseFloat(rgbMatch[7]) : 1\n        return createColor(r, g, b, a)\n    }\n\n    return null\n}\n\n/**\n * Parse a length value for transform properties (no keyword support)\n * number \u2192 Length(n, Pixel), \"50%\" \u2192 Length(50, Percent), \"10px\" \u2192 Length(10, Pixel)\n */\nfunction parseLengthForTransform(value: number | string): CSLength | null {\n    if (typeof value === \"number\") {\n        return new CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.Length(value, CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.LengthUnit.Pixel)\n    }\n    if (typeof value === \"string\") {\n        const match = value.trim().match(/^(-?[\\d.]+)(px|%)?$/)\n        if (match) {\n            const num = parseFloat(match[1])\n            if (isNaN(num)) return null\n            const unit = match[2] === \"%\"\n                ? CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.LengthUnit.Percent\n                : CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.LengthUnit.Pixel\n            return new CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.Length(num, unit)\n        }\n    }\n    return null\n}\n\n/**\n * Parse an angle value\n * number \u2192 Angle.Degrees(n), string \u2192 parse unit suffix (deg|rad|turn|grad)\n */\nfunction parseAngle(value: number | string): any | null {\n    const Angle = CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.Angle\n    if (typeof value === \"number\") {\n        return Angle.Degrees(value)\n    }\n    if (typeof value === \"string\") {\n        const match = value.trim().match(/^(-?[\\d.]+)(deg|rad|turn|grad)?$/)\n        if (match) {\n            const num = parseFloat(match[1])\n            if (isNaN(num)) return null\n            const unit = match[2] || \"deg\"\n            if (unit === \"rad\") return Angle.Radians(num)\n            if (unit === \"turn\") return Angle.Turns(num)\n            if (unit === \"grad\") return Angle.Gradians(num)\n            return Angle.Degrees(num)\n        }\n    }\n    return null\n}\n\n/**\n * Parse translate style: [x, y] array or C# Translate pass-through\n */\nfunction parseTranslateStyle(value: unknown): unknown {\n    if (Array.isArray(value)) {\n        const x = parseLengthForTransform(value[0])\n        const y = parseLengthForTransform(value[1])\n        if (x !== null && y !== null) {\n            return new CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.StyleTranslate(\n                new CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.Translate(x, y)\n            )\n        }\n    }\n    if (typeof value === \"object\" && value !== null) {\n        try { return new CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.StyleTranslate(value) } catch { return value }\n    }\n    return value\n}\n\n/**\n * Parse rotate style: number (degrees), string with unit, or C# Rotate pass-through\n */\nfunction parseRotateStyle(value: unknown): unknown {\n    if (typeof value === \"number\" || typeof value === \"string\") {\n        const angle = parseAngle(value)\n        if (angle !== null) {\n            return new CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.StyleRotate(\n                new CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.Rotate(angle)\n            )\n        }\n    }\n    if (typeof value === \"object\" && value !== null) {\n        try { return new CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.StyleRotate(value) } catch { return value }\n    }\n    return value\n}\n\n/**\n * Parse scale style: number (uniform), [x, y] array, or C# Scale pass-through\n */\nfunction parseScaleStyle(value: unknown): unknown {\n    if (typeof value === \"number\") {\n        return new CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.StyleScale(\n            new CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.Scale(new CS.UnityEngine.Vector3(value, value, 1))\n        )\n    }\n    if (Array.isArray(value)) {\n        const x = typeof value[0] === \"number\" ? value[0] : 1\n        const y = typeof value[1] === \"number\" ? value[1] : 1\n        return new CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.StyleScale(\n            new CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.Scale(new CS.UnityEngine.Vector3(x, y, 1))\n        )\n    }\n    if (typeof value === \"object\" && value !== null) {\n        try { return new CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.StyleScale(value) } catch { return value }\n    }\n    return value\n}\n\n/**\n * Parse transformOrigin style: [x, y] array or C# TransformOrigin pass-through\n */\nfunction parseTransformOriginStyle(value: unknown): unknown {\n    if (Array.isArray(value)) {\n        const x = parseLengthForTransform(value[0])\n        const y = parseLengthForTransform(value[1])\n        if (x !== null && y !== null) {\n            return new CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.StyleTransformOrigin(\n                new CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.TransformOrigin(x, y)\n            )\n        }\n    }\n    if (typeof value === \"object\" && value !== null) {\n        try { return new CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.StyleTransformOrigin(value) } catch { return value }\n    }\n    return value\n}\n\n/**\n * Parse a style value based on the property name\n * @param key - Style property name (e.g., \"width\", \"backgroundColor\")\n * @param value - Raw value from React style object\n * @returns Parsed value suitable for Unity UI Toolkit\n */\nexport function parseStyleValue(key: string, value: unknown): unknown {\n    // `unityMaterial` is special: it can legitimately be `null` (meaning\n    // \"reset to the panel default\") so we can't short-circuit like the\n    // length/color paths below. Wrap in StyleMaterialDefinition either way.\n    if (key === \"unityMaterial\") {\n        if (value === undefined) return undefined\n        const SMD = CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.StyleMaterialDefinition\n        if (value === null) {\n            return new SMD(CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.StyleKeyword.Null)\n        }\n        return new SMD(value as any)\n    }\n\n    if (value === undefined || value === null) return value\n\n    // Length properties\n    if (LENGTH_PROPERTIES.has(key)) {\n        if (typeof value === \"number\") {\n            return new CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.Length(value, CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.LengthUnit.Pixel)\n        }\n        if (typeof value === \"string\") {\n            const parsed = parseLength(value)\n            if (parsed !== null) return parsed\n        }\n        // Fall through to return original value\n    }\n\n    // Color properties\n    if (COLOR_PROPERTIES.has(key)) {\n        if (typeof value === \"string\") {\n            const parsed = parseColor(value)\n            if (parsed !== null) return parsed\n        }\n        // Could already be a Color object, pass through\n    }\n\n    // Plain number properties - pass through as-is\n    if (NUMBER_PROPERTIES.has(key)) {\n        return value\n    }\n\n    // Enum properties - convert string to Unity enum value\n    if (key in ENUM_MAPPINGS && typeof value === \"string\") {\n        const parsed = parseEnumValue(key, value)\n        if (parsed !== null) return parsed\n        // Fall through if parsing failed\n    }\n\n    // Transform properties\n    if (key === \"translate\") return parseTranslateStyle(value)\n    if (key === \"rotate\") return parseRotateStyle(value)\n    if (key === \"scale\") return parseScaleStyle(value)\n    if (key === \"transformOrigin\") return parseTransformOriginStyle(value)\n\n    // Unknown property - pass through unchanged\n    return value\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if a property is a length property\n */\nexport function isLengthProperty(key: string): boolean {\n    return LENGTH_PROPERTIES.has(key)\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if a property is a color property\n */\nexport 'borderLeftWidth'],\n    borderColor: ['borderTopColor', 'borderRightColor', 'borderBottomColor', 'borderLeftColor'],\n    borderRadius: ['borderTopLeftRadius', 'borderTopRightRadius', 'borderBottomRightRadius', 'borderBottomLeftRadius'],\n};\n\n// Character escape mapping for Tailwind/USS compatibility\n// USS class names only support [\\w-] so special characters must be escaped\nconst CLASS_ESCAPE_MAP: [string, string][] = [\n    [':', '_c_'],   // Pseudo-classes and breakpoints (hover:, sm:)\n    ['/', '_s_'],   // Fractions (w-1/2)\n    ['.', '_d_'],   // Decimals (p-2.5)\n    ['[', '_lb_'],  // Arbitrary values ([100px])\n    [']', '_rb_'],\n    ['(', '_lp_'],  // Functions (calc())\n    [')', '_rp_'],\n    ['#', '_n_'],   // Hex colors\n    ['%', '_p_'],   // Percentages\n    [',', '_cm_'],  // Multiple values\n    ['&', '_amp_'],\n    ['>', '_gt_'],\n    ['<', '_lt_'],\n    ['*', '_ast_'],\n    [\"'\", '_sq_'],\n];\n\n/**\n * Escape special characters in a class name for USS compatibility.\n * Tailwind class names like \"hover:bg-red-500\" become \"hover_c_bg-red-500\"\n * Results are cached since the same class names are used repeatedly across renders.\n */\nconst _escapeCache = new Map<string, string>();\nfunction escapeClassName(name: string): string {\n    const cached = _escapeCache.get(name);\n    if (cached !== undefined) return cached;\n    let escaped = name;\n    for (const [char, replacement] of CLASS_ESCAPE_MAP) {\n        if (escaped.includes(char)) {\n            escaped = escaped.split(char).join(replacement);\n        }\n    }\n    _escapeCache.set(name, escaped);\n    return escaped;\n}\n\n// Shallow equality check for plain objects (style, props).\n// Returns true if both have identical own-property values (===).\nfunction shallowEqual(a: Record<string, unknown> | undefined, b: Record<string, unknown> | undefined): boolean {\n    if (a === b) return true;\n    if (!a || !b) return false;\n    const keysA = Object.keys(a);\n    const keysB = Object.keys(b);\n    if (keysA.length !== keysB.length) return false;\n    for (let i = 0; i < keysA.length; i++) {\n        const k = keysA[i];\n        if (a[k] !== b[k]) return false;\n    }\n    return true;\n}\n\n// Get all expanded property keys for a style object\nfunction getExpandedStyleKeys(style: ViewStyle | undefined): Set<string> {\n    const keys = new Set<string>();\n    if (!style) return keys;\n\n    for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(style)) {\n        if (value === undefined) continue;\n\n        const expanded = STYLE_SHORTHANDS[key];\n        if (expanded) {\n            for (const prop of expanded) {\n                keys.add(prop);\n            }\n        } else {\n            keys.add(key);\n        }\n    }\n    return keys;\n}\n\n/**\n * RenderTexture-like object for backgroundImage style property.\n * Can be either:\n * - A marker object with __rtHandle (from rt.getUnityObject())\n * - A RenderTexture object directly (has __handle property)\n */\ninterface RenderTextureRef {\n    __rtHandle?: number;\n    __handle?: number;\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if a value is a RenderTexture or RenderTexture handle marker.\n * Supports both:\n * - Direct RenderTexture objects (have __handle)\n * - Marker objects from getUnityObject() (have __rtHandle)\n */\nfunction isRenderTextureHandle(value: unknown): value is RenderTextureRef {\n    if (typeof value !== \"object\" || value === null) return false;\n    return \"__rtHandle\" in value || \"__handle\" in value;\n}\n\n/**\n * Get the RT handle from a RenderTexture-like object.\n */\nfunction getRenderTextureHandle(value: RenderTextureRef): number {\n    return value.__rtHandle ?? value.__handle ?? -1;\n}\n\n// Apply style properties to element, returns the set of applied keys\nfunction applyStyle(element: CSObject, style: ViewStyle | undefined): Set<string> {\n    const appliedKeys = new Set<string>();\n    if (!style) return appliedKeys;\n\n    const s = element.style;\n    for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(style)) {\n        if (value === undefined) continue;\n\n        // Handle shorthand properties\n        const expanded = STYLE_SHORTHANDS[key];\n        if (expanded) {\n            // Parse the value once, apply to all expanded properties\n            const parsed = parseStyleValue(expanded[0], value);\n            for (const prop of expanded) {\n                s[prop] = parsed;\n                appliedKeys.add(prop);\n            }\n        } else if (key === \"backgroundImage\") {\n            if (value == null) {\n                CS.OneJS.GPU.GPUBridge.ClearElementBackgroundImage(element);\n            } else if (isRenderTextureHandle(value)) {\n                // GPU compute RenderTexture handles (via rt.getUnityObject())\n                const handle = getRenderTextureHandle(value);\n                if (handle >= 0) {\n                    CS.OneJS.GPU.GPUBridge.SetElementBackgroundImage(element, handle);\n                }\n            } else if (typeof value === \"object\" && \"__csHandle\" in value) {\n                // C# objects: Texture2D, Sprite, VectorImage, RenderTexture\n                CS.OneJS.GPU.GPUBridge.SetElementBackgroundFromObject(element, value);\n            }\n            appliedKeys.add(key);\n        } else {\n            // Parse and apply individual property\n            s[key] = parseStyleValue(key, value);\n            appliedKeys.add(key);\n        }\n    }\n    return appliedKeys;\n}\n\n// Clear style properties that are no longer in the new style\nfunction clearRemovedStyles(element: CSObject, oldKeys: Set<string>, newKeys: Set<string>) {\n    const s = element.style;\n    for (const key of oldKeys) {\n        if (!newKeys.has(key)) {\n            if (key === \"backgroundImage\") {\n                // Special handling for backgroundImage - use GPUBridge to clear\n                CS.OneJS.GPU.GPUBridge.ClearElementBackgroundImage(element);\n            } else {\n                // Setting to undefined clears the inline style, falling back to USS\n                s[key] = undefined;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// Parse className string into a Set of escaped class names.\n// Results are cached since the same className strings recur across renders.\nconst _parseCache = new Map<string, Set<string>>();\nfunction parseClassNames(className: string | undefined): Set<string> {\n    if (!className) return new Set();\n    const cached = _parseCache.get(className);\n    if (cached !== undefined) return cached;\n    const result = new Set(\n        className.split(/\\s+/)\n            .filter(Boolean)\n            .map(escapeClassName)\n    );\n    _parseCache.set(className, result);\n    return result;\n}\n\n// Apply className(s) to element (with escaping for Tailwind/USS compatibility)\nfunction applyClassName(element: CSObject, className: string | undefined) {\n    if (!className) return;\n\n    const classes = className.split(/\\s+/).filter(Boolean);\n    for (const cls of classes) {\n        element.AddToClassList(escapeClassName(cls));\n    }\n}\n\n// Update className selectively - only add/remove what changed\nfunction updateClassNames(element: CSObject, oldClassName: string | undefined, newClassName: string | undefined) {\n    const oldClasses = parseClassNames(oldClassName);\n    const newClasses = parseClassNames(newClassName);\n\n    // Remove classes that are no longer present\n    for (const cls of oldClasses) {\n        if (!newClasses.has(cls)) {\n            element.RemoveFromClassList(cls);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Add classes that are new\n    for (const cls of newClasses) {\n        if (!oldClasses.has(cls)) {\n            element.AddToClassList(cls);\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// Track parent-child relationships for event bubbling\nfunction trackParent(child: CSObject, parent: CSObject) {\n    const childHandle = (child as unknown as { __csHandle: number }).__csHandle;\n    const parentHandle = (parent as unknown as { __csHandle: number }).__csHandle;\n    if (childHandle > 0 && parentHandle > 0) {\n        __eventAPI.setParent(childHandle, parentHandle);\n    }\n}\n\nfunction untrackParent(child: CSObject) {\n    const childHandle = (child as unknown as { __csHandle: number }).__csHandle;\n    if (childHandle > 0) {\n        __eventAPI.removeParent(childHandle);\n    }\n}\n\n\n// Apply event handlers\nfunction applyEvents(instance: Instance, props: BaseProps) {\n    for (const [propName, eventType] of Object.entries(EVENT_PROPS)) {\n        const handler = (props as Record<string, unknown>)[propName] as Function | undefined;\n        const existingHandler = instance.eventHandlers.get(eventType);\n\n        if (handler !== existingHandler) {\n            if (existingHandler) {\n                __eventAPI.removeEventListener(instance.element, eventType, existingHandler);\n                instance.eventHandlers.delete(eventType);\n            }\n            if (handler) {\n                __eventAPI.addEventListener(instance.element, eventType, handler);\n                instance.eventHandlers.set(eventType, handler);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/**\n * Apply generateVisualContent callback for vector drawing.\n * Uses Unity's generateVisualContent delegate on VisualElement.\n *\n * This follows the same pattern as ListView's makeItem/bindItem callbacks -\n * we assign JS functions directly to C# delegate properties via the interop layer.\n */\nfunction applyVisualContentCallback(instance: Instance, props: BaseProps) {\n    const callback = props.onGenerateVisualContent;\n    const existingCallback = instance.visualContentCallback;\n\n    if (callback !== existingCallback) {\n        const element = instance.element as unknown as { generateVisualContent: GenerateVisualContentCallback | null };\n\n        // Remove old callback if exists\n        if (existingCallback) {\n            // Clear the delegate via C# interop\n            element.generateVisualContent = null;\n        }\n\n        // Add new callback if provided\n        if (callback) {\n            // Assign callback to generateVisualContent property\n            // The C# interop layer handles the delegate conversion\n            element.generateVisualContent = callback;\n            instance.visualContentCallback = callback;\n        } else {\n            instance.visualContentCallback = undefined;\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// MARK: Text Merging\n// Rebuild concatenated text from merged text children\nfunction rebuildMergedText(instance: Instance) {\n    const children = instance.mergedTextChildren;\n    if (!children || children.length === 0) {\n        // No merged children - clear text (or keep prop-based text?)\n        // For now, set to empty - if user wants text, they should use children\n        instance.element.text = '';\n        return;\n    }\n    instance.element.text = children.map(c => c.element.text || '').join('');\n}\n\n// Check if a child should be merged into parent's text property\nfunction shouldMergeText(parentInstance: Instance, child: Instance): boolean {\n    // Don't merge if parent has mixed content (non-text children were added)\n    if (parentInstance.hasMixedContent) return false;\n    return TEXT_MERGE_TYPES.has(parentInstance.type) && child.type === 'text';\n}\n\n// \"Unmerge\" all text children - add them as actual visual children\n// Called when a non-text child is added, breaking the pure-text assumption\nfunction unmergTextChildren(parentInstance: Instance) {\n    const children = parentInstance.mergedTextChildren;\n    if (!children || children.length === 0) return;\n\n    // Clear parent's merged text\n    parentInstance.element.text = '';\n\n    // Add each merged text child as an actual visual child\n    for (const child of children) {\n        child.mergedInto = undefined;\n        parentInstance.element.Add(child.element);\n    }\n\n    // Clear the merged children list\n    parentInstance.mergedTextChildren = undefined;\n    parentInstance.hasMixedContent = true;\n}\n\n// Handle adding a non-text child to a text-merge parent\n// This triggers unmerging of any previously merged text\nfunction handleNonTextChild(parentInstance: Instance) {\n    if (TEXT_MERGE_TYPES.has(parentInstance.type) && !parentInstance.hasMixedContent) {\n        unmergTextChildren(parentInstance);\n    }\n}\n\n// Append a text child to a text-merging parent\nfunction appendMergedTextChild(parentInstance: Instance, child: Instance) {\n    if (!parentInstance.mergedTextChildren) {\n        parentInstance.mergedTextChildren = [];\n    }\n    parentInstance.mergedTextChildren.push(child);\n    child.mergedInto = parentInstance;\n    rebuildMergedText(parentInstance);\n}\n\n// Insert a text child before another in a text-merging parent\nfunction insertMergedTextChild(parentInstance: Instance, child: Instance, beforeChild: Instance) {\n    if (!parentInstance.mergedTextChildren) {\n        parentInstance.mergedTextChildren = [];\n    }\n    const index = parentInstance.mergedTextChildren.indexOf(beforeChild);\n    if (index >= 0) {\n        parentInstance.mergedTextChildren.splice(index, 0, child);\n    } else {\n        parentInstance.mergedTextChildren.push(child);\n    }\n    child.mergedInto = parentInstance;\n    rebuildMergedText(parentInstance);\n}\n\n// Remove a text child from a text-merging parent\nfunction removeMergedTextChild(parentInstance: Instance, child: Instance) {\n    const children = parentInstance.mergedTextChildren;\n    if (children) {\n        const index = children.indexOf(child);\n        if (index >= 0) {\n            children.splice(index, 1);\n        }\n    }\n    child.mergedInto = undefined;\n    rebuildMergedText(parentInstance);\n}\n\n// MARK: Component-specific prop handlers\n\n// Apply common props (text, value, label) - skip unchanged values\nfunction applyCommonProps(element: CSObject, props: Record<string, unknown>, oldProps?: Record<string, unknown>) {\n    const el = element as any;\n    if (props.text !== undefined && props.text !== oldProps?.text) el.text = props.text as string;\n    if (props.value !== undefined && props.value !== oldProps?.value) el.value = props.value;\n    if (props.label !== undefined && props.label !== oldProps?.label) el.label = props.label as string;\n}\n\n// Helper to set enum prop if defined and changed\nfunction setEnumProp<T>(target: T, key: keyof T, props: Record<string, unknown>, propKey: string, enumType: CSEnum, oldProps?: Record<string, unknown>) {\n    if (props[propKey] !== undefined && props[propKey] !== oldProps?.[propKey]) {\n        (target as Record<string, unknown>)[key as string] = enumType[props[propKey] as string];\n    }\n}\n\n// Helper to set value prop if defined and changed\nfunction setValueProp<T>(target: T, key: keyof T, props: Record<string, unknown>, propKey: string, oldProps?: Record<string, unknown>) {\n    if (props[propKey] !== undefined && props[propKey] !== oldProps?.[propKey]) {\n        (target as Record<string, unknown>)[key as string] = props[propKey];\n    }\n}\n\n// Apply TextField-specific properties - skip unchanged values\nfunction applyTextFieldProps(element: CSObject, props: Record<string, unknown>, oldProps?: Record<string, unknown>) {\n    const el = element as any;\n    if (props.readOnly !== undefined && props.readOnly !== oldProps?.readOnly) el.isReadOnly = props.readOnly;\n    if (props.multiline !== undefined && props.multiline !== oldProps?.multiline) el.multiline = props.multiline;\n    if (props.maxLength !== undefined && props.maxLength !== oldProps?.maxLength) el.maxLength = props.maxLength;\n    if (props.isPasswordField !== undefined && props.isPasswordField !== oldProps?.isPasswordField) el.isPasswordField = props.isPasswordField;\n    if (props.maskChar !== undefined && props.maskChar !== oldProps?.maskChar) el.maskChar = (props.maskChar as string).charAt(0);\n    if (props.isDelayed !== undefined && props.isDelayed !== oldProps?.isDelayed) el.isDelayed = props.isDelayed;\n    if (props.selectAllOnFocus !== undefined && props.selectAllOnFocus !== oldProps?.selectAllOnFocus) el.selectAllOnFocus = props.selectAllOnFocus;\n    if (props.selectAllOnMouseUp !== undefined && props.selectAllOnMouseUp !== oldProps?.selectAllOnMouseUp) el.selectAllOnMouseUp = props.selectAllOnMouseUp;\n    if (props.hideMobileInput !== undefined && props.hideMobileInput !== oldProps?.hideMobileInput) el.hideMobileInput = props.hideMobileInput;\n    if (props.autoCorrection !== undefined && props.autoCorrection !== oldProps?.autoCorrection) el.autoCorrection = props.autoCorrection;\n}\n\n// Apply Slider-specific properties - skip unchanged values\nfunction applySliderProps(element: CSObject, props: Record<string, unknown>, oldProps?: Record<string, unknown>) {\n    const el = element as any;\n    if (props.lowValue !== undefined && props.lowValue !== oldProps?.lowValue) el.lowValue = props.lowValue;\n    if (props.highValue !== undefined && props.highValue !== oldProps?.highValue) el.highValue = props.highValue;\n    if (props.showInputField !== undefined && props.showInputField !== oldProps?.showInputField) el.showInputField = props.showInputField;\n    if (props.inverted !== undefined && props.inverted !== oldProps?.inverted) el.inverted = props.inverted;\n    if (props.pageSize !== undefined && props.pageSize !== oldProps?.pageSize) el.pageSize = props.pageSize;\n    if (props.fill !== undefined && props.fill !== oldProps?.fill) el.fill = props.fill;\n    if (props.direction !== undefined && props.direction !== oldProps?.direction) {\n        el.direction = CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.SliderDirection[props.direction as string];\n    }\n}\n\n// Apply Toggle-specific properties - skip unchanged values\nfunction applyToggleProps(element: CSObject, props: Record<string, unknown>, oldProps?: Record<string, unknown>) {\n    const el = element as any;\n    if (props.text !== undefined && props.text !== oldProps?.text) el.text = props.text;\n    if (props.toggleOnLabelClick !== undefined && props.toggleOnLabelClick !== oldProps?.toggleOnLabelClick) el.toggleOnLabelClick = props.toggleOnLabelClick;\n}\n\n// Apply Image-specific properties - skip unchanged values\nfunction applyImageProps(element: CSObject, props: Record<string, unknown>, oldProps?: Record<string, unknown>) {\n    const el = element as any;\n    if (props.image !== undefined && props.image !== oldProps?.image) {\n        const img = props.image;\n        if (img != null && (img as any).GetType?.().Name === \"VectorImage\") {\n            el.image = null;\n            el.vectorImage = img;\n        } else {\n            el.vectorImage = null;\n            el.image = img;\n        }\n    }\n    if (props.sprite !== undefined && props.sprite !== oldProps?.sprite) el.sprite = props.sprite;\n    if (props.vectorImage !== undefined && props.vectorImage !== oldProps?.vectorImage) el.vectorImage = props.vectorImage;\n    if (props.scaleMode !== undefined && props.scaleMode !== oldProps?.scaleMode) {\n        el.scaleMode = CS.UnityEngine.ScaleMode[props.scaleMode as string];\n    }\n    if (props.tintColor !== undefined && props.tintColor !== oldProps?.tintColor) {\n        const color = parseColor(props.tintColor as string);\n        if (color) el.tintColor = color;\n    }\n    if (props.sourceRect !== undefined && props.sourceRect !== oldProps?.sourceRect) {\n        const rect = props.sourceRect as { x: number; y: number; width: number; height: number };\n        el.sourceRect = new CS.UnityEngine.Rect(rect.x, rect.y, rect.width, rect.height);\n    }\n    if (props.uv !== undefined && props.uv !== oldProps?.uv) {\n        const rect = props.uv as { x: number; y: number; width: number; height: number };\n        el.uv = new CS.UnityEngine.Rect(rect.x, rect.y, rect.width, rect.height);\n    }\n}\n\n// Apply ScrollView-specific properties - skip unchanged values\nfunction applyScrollViewProps(element: CSScrollView, props: Record<string, unknown>, oldProps?: Record<string, unknown>) {\n    const UIE = CS.UnityEngine.UIElements;\n    setEnumProp(element, 'mode', props, 'mode', UIE.ScrollViewMode, oldProps);\n    setEnumProp(element, 'horizontalScrollerVisibility', props, 'horizontalScrollerVisibility', UIE.ScrollerVisibility, oldProps);\n    setEnumProp(element, 'verticalScrollerVisibility', props, 'verticalScrollerVisibility', UIE.ScrollerVisibility, oldProps);\n    setEnumProp(element, 'touchScrollBehavior', props, 'touchScrollBehavior', UIE.TouchScrollBehavior, oldProps);\n    setEnumProp(element, 'nestedInteractionKind', props, 'nestedInteractionKind', UIE.NestedInteractionKind, oldProps);\n    setValueProp(element, 'elasticity', props, 'elasticity', oldProps);\n    setValueProp(element, 'elasticAnimationIntervalMs', props, 'elasticAnimationIntervalMs', oldProps);\n    setValueProp(element, 'scrollDecelerationRate', props, 'scrollDecelerationRate', oldProps);\n    setValueProp(element, 'mouseWheelScrollSize', props, 'mouseWheelScrollSize', oldProps);\n    setValueProp(element, 'horizontalPageSize', props, 'horizontalPageSize', oldProps);\n    setValueProp(element, 'verticalPageSize', props, 'verticalPageSize', oldProps);\n}\n\n// Apply ListView-specific properties - skip unchanged values\nfunction applyListViewProps(element: CSListView, props: Record<string, unknown>, oldProps?: Record<string, unknown>) {\n    const UIE = CS.UnityEngine.UIElements;\n\n    // Data binding callbacks\n    setValueProp(element, 'itemsSource', props, 'itemsSource', oldProps);\n    setValueProp(element, 'makeItem', props, 'makeItem', oldProps);\n    setValueProp(element, 'bindItem', props, 'bindItem', oldProps);\n    setValueProp(element, 'unbindItem', props, 'unbindItem', oldProps);\n    setValueProp(element, 'destroyItem', props, 'destroyItem', oldProps);\n\n    // Virtualization\n    setValueProp(element, 'fixedItemHeight', props, 'fixedItemHeight', oldProps);\n    setEnumProp(element, 'virtualizationMethod', props, 'virtualizationMethod', UIE.CollectionVirtualizationMethod, oldProps);\n\n    // Selection\n    setEnumProp(element, 'selectionType', props, 'selectionType', UIE.SelectionType, oldProps);\n    setValueProp(element, 'selectedIndex', props, 'selectedIndex', oldProps);\n    setValueProp(element, 'selectedIndices', props, 'selectedIndices', oldProps);\n\n    // Reordering\n    setValueProp(element, 'reorderable', props, 'reorderable', oldProps);\n    setEnumProp(element, 'reorderMode', props, 'reorderMode', UIE.ListViewReorderMode, oldProps);\n\n    // Header/Footer\n    setValueProp(element, 'showFoldoutHeader', props, 'showFoldoutHeader', oldProps);\n    setValueProp(element, 'headerTitle', props, 'headerTitle', oldProps);\n    setValueProp(element, 'showAddRemoveFooter', props, 'showAddRemoveFooter', oldProps);\n\n    // Appearance\n    setValueProp(element, 'showBorder', props, 'showBorder', oldProps);\n    setEnumProp(element, 'showAlternatingRowBackgrounds', props, 'showAlternatingRowBackgrounds', UIE.AlternatingRowBackground, oldProps);\n}\n\n// Props handled by the reconciler infrastructure - not forwarded to C# elements\nconst RESERVED_PROPS = new Set([\n    'children', 'key', 'ref', 'style', 'className', 'pickingMode', 'focusable', 'disabled',\n    'onGenerateVisualContent',\n    ...Object.keys(EVENT_PROPS),\n]);\n\n// Forward non-reserved props directly to C# element (for custom elements) - skip unchanged\nfunction applyCustomProps(element: CSObject, props: Record<string, unknown>, oldProps?: Record<string, unknown>) {\n    for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(props)) {\n        if (value === undefined || RESERVED_PROPS.has(key)) continue;\n        if (value === oldProps?.[key]) continue;\n        (element as any)[key] = value;\n    }\n}\n\n// Apply component-specific props based on element type\nfunction applyComponentProps(element: CSObject, type: string, props: Record<string, unknown>, oldProps?: Record<string, unknown>) {\n    // Custom elements: forward all non-reserved props directly to C# element\n    if (!BUILT_IN_TYPES.has(type)) {\n        applyCustomProps(element, props, oldProps);\n        return;\n    }\n\n    // For Slider, apply range props (lowValue/highValue) BEFORE value\n    // Unity's Slider clamps value to [lowValue, highValue], so range must be set first\n    if (type === 'ojs-slider') {\n        applySliderProps(element, props, oldProps);\n        applyCommonProps(element, props, oldProps);\n        return;\n    }\n\n    applyCommonProps(element, props, oldProps);\n\n    if (type === 'ojs-textfield') {\n        applyTextFieldProps(element, props, oldProps);\n    } else if (type === 'ojs-toggle') {\n        applyToggleProps(element, props, oldProps);\n    } else if (type === 'ojs-image') {\n        applyImageProps(element, props, oldProps);\n    } else if (type === 'ojs-scrollview') {\n        applyScrollViewProps(element as CSScrollView, props, oldProps);\n    } else if (type === 'ojs-listview') {\n        applyListViewProps(element as CSListView, props, oldProps);\n    } else if (type === 'ojs-frostedglass') {\n        const el = element as any;\n        if (props.blurRadius !== undefined && props.blurRadius !== oldProps?.blurRadius) el.BlurRadius = props.blurRadius;\n        if (props.tintColor !== undefined && props.tintColor !== oldProps?.tintColor) el.TintColor = props.tintColor;\n    }\n}\n\n// Create an instance\nfunction createInstance(type: string, props: BaseProps): Instance {\n    const factory = TYPE_MAP[type];\n    if (!factory) {\n        throw new Error(`Unknown element type: ${type}`);\n    }\n\n    const element = factory();\n    const appliedStyleKeys = applyStyle(element, props.style);\n    const instance: Instance = {\n        element,\n        type,\n        props,\n        eventHandlers: new Map(),\n        appliedStyleKeys,\n    };\n\n    applyClassName(element, props.className);\n    applyEvents(instance, props);\n    applyVisualContentCallback(instance, props);\n    applyComponentProps(element, type, props as Record<string, unknown>);\n\n    // Apply pickingMode\n    if (props.pickingMode !== undefined) {\n        element.pickingMode = CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.PickingMode[props.pickingMode];\n    }\n\n    // Apply focusable\n    if (props.focusable !== undefined) {\n        element.focusable = props.focusable;\n    }\n\n    // Apply disabled (inverted \u2014 SetEnabled(true) means \"not disabled\")\n    if (props.disabled !== undefined) {\n        element.SetEnabled(!props.disabled);\n    }\n\n    return instance;\n}\n\n// Update an instance with new props\nfunction updateInstance(instance: Instance, oldProps: BaseProps, newProps: BaseProps) {\n    const element = instance.element;\n\n    // Update style - skip if values are shallowly equal (inline style objects are\n    // new references each render but usually contain the same values)\n    if (oldProps.style !== newProps.style && !shallowEqual(oldProps.style as any, newProps.style as any)) {\n        const newStyleKeys = getExpandedStyleKeys(newProps.style);\n        clearRemovedStyles(element, instance.appliedStyleKeys, newStyleKeys);\n        instance.appliedStyleKeys = applyStyle(element, newProps.style);\n    }\n\n    // Update className - selectively add/remove classes\n    if (oldProps.className !== newProps.className) {\n        updateClassNames(element, oldProps.className, newProps.className);\n    }\n\n    // Update events\n    applyEvents(instance, newProps);\n\n    // Update vector drawing callback\n    applyVisualContentCallback(instance, newProps);\n\n    // Update component-specific props - pass oldProps to skip unchanged values\n    applyComponentProps(element, instance.type, newProps as Record<string, unknown>, oldProps as Record<string, unknown>);\n\n    // Update pickingMode\n    if (oldProps.pickingMode !== newProps.pickingMode) {\n        if (newProps.pickingMode !== undefined) {\n            element.pickingMode = CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.PickingMode[newProps.pickingMode];\n        } else {\n            // Reset to default (Position)\n            element.pickingMode = CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.PickingMode.Position;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Update focusable - only set if explicitly provided, do not override\n    // element-specific defaults (Button is focusable by default, View is not)\n    // when the prop is removed.\n    if (oldProps.focusable !== newProps.focusable && newProps.focusable !== undefined) {\n        element.focusable = newProps.focusable;\n    }\n\n    // Update disabled - unlike focusable, every VisualElement starts enabled\n    // by default, so removing the prop after a previous `disabled={true}` is\n    // expected to restore the enabled state.\n    if (oldProps.disabled !== newProps.disabled) {\n        if (newProps.disabled !== undefined) {\n            element.SetEnabled(!newProps.disabled);\n        } else {\n            element.SetEnabled(true);\n        }\n    }\n\n    instance.props = newProps;\n}\n\n// NOTE: We use a type assertion because @types/react-reconciler (0.28.x) is outdated\n// and doesn't match react-reconciler 0.31.x (React 19). typing.\ntype OurHostConfig = HostConfig<\n    string,           // Type\n    BaseProps,        // Props\n    Container,        // Container\n    Instance,         // Instance\n    TextInstance,     // TextInstance\n    never,            // SuspenseInstance\n    never,            // HydratableInstance\n    CSObject,         // PublicInstance - refs point to the actual UI Toolkit element\n    {},               // HostContext\n    true,             // UpdatePayload (true = needs update)\n    ChildSet,         // ChildSet\n    number,           // TimeoutHandle\n    number            // isPrimaryRenderer: true,\n    noTimeout: -1,\n\n    createInstance(type: string, props: BaseProps) {\n        return createInstance(type, props);\n    },\n\n    createTextInstance(text: string) {\n        // Create a TextElement for implicit text content\n        // Using TextElement (not Label) for semantic clarity:\n        // - TextElement = raw text content in JSX\n        // - Label = explicit (shouldMergeText(parentInstance, child)) {\n            appendMergedTextChild(parentInstance, child);\n        } else {\n            handleNonTextChild(parentInstance);\n parentInstance.element.Add(child.element);\n        }\n        trackParent(child.element, parentInstance.element);\n    },\n\n    appendChildToContainer(container: Container, child: Instance) {\n (shouldMergeText(parentInstance, child)) {\n            insertMergedTextChild(parentInstance, child, beforeChild);\n        } else {\n            handleNonTextChild(parentInstance);\n            const BaseProps, _fiber: unknown) {\n        updateInstance(instance, oldProps, newProps);\n    },\n\n    commitTextUpdate(textInstance: Instance, _oldText: string, newText: string) {\n updates before returning.\n * Useful for tests or when you need immediate UI updates.\n */\nexport function flushSync<T>(callback: () => T): T {\n    if (typeof (reconciler as any).flushSync === 'function') {\n        return (reconciler as any).flushSync(callback);\n    }\n    // Fallback: just call the callback\n    return callback();\n}\n\n/**\n * Batch multiple updates together for better performance.\n * All updates inside the callback are batched into a single render.\n */\nexport function batchedUpdates<T>(callback: () => T): T {\n    if (typeof (reconciler as any).batchedUpdates === 'function') {\n        return (reconciler as any).batchedUpdates(callback);\n    }\n    // Fallback: just call the callback\n    return callback();\n}\n\n// Export for testing/debugging\nexport ReactNode, type ErrorInfo } from \"react\"\nimport { View, Label } from \"./components\"\n\ndeclare const console: { log: (...args: unknown[]) => void; error: (...args: unknown[]) => void }\n\nexport interface ErrorBoundaryProps {\n    children: ReactNode\n    /**\n     * Optional fallback to render when an error occurs.\n     * Can be a ReactNode or a function that receives error details.\n     */\n    fallback?: ReactNode | ((error: Error, errorInfo: ErrorInfo) => ReactNode)\n    /**\n     * Optional callback when an error is caught.\n     * Use this for logging or error reporting.\n     */\n    onError?: (error: Error, errorInfo: ErrorInfo) => void\n    /**\n     * Optional callback when the boundary resets.\n     */\n    onReset?: () => void\n}\n\ninterface ErrorBoundaryState {\n    hasError: boolean\n    error: Error | null\n    errorInfo: ErrorInfo | null\n}\n\n/**\n * Error boundary component for catching and displaying React errors.\n *\n * @example Basic usage with default fallback\n * ```tsx\n * <ErrorBoundary>\n *   <MyComponent />\n * </ErrorBoundary>\n * ```\n *\n * @example Custom fallback UI\n * ```tsx\n * <ErrorBoundary fallback={<Label>Something went wrong</Label>}>\n *   <MyComponent />\n * </ErrorBoundary>\n * ```\n *\n * @example Fallback function with error details\n * ```tsx\n * <ErrorBoundary\n *   fallback={(error, info) => (\n *     <View>\n *       <Label>Error: {error.message}</Label>\n *       <Label>Stack: {info.componentStack}</Label>\n *     </View>\n *   )}\n * >\n *   <MyComponent />\n * </ErrorBoundary>\n * ```\n *\n * @example Error logging\n * ```tsx\n * <ErrorBoundary onError={(error, info) => logError(error, info)}>\n *   <MyComponent />\n * </ErrorBoundary>\n * ```\n */\nexport class ErrorBoundary extends Component<ErrorBoundaryProps, ErrorBoundaryState> {\n    constructor(props: ErrorBoundaryProps) {\n        super(props)\n        this.state = {\n            hasError: false,\n            error: null,\n            errorInfo: null,\n        }\n    }\n\n    static getDerivedStateFromError(error: Error): Partial<ErrorBoundaryState> {\n        return { hasError: true, error }\n    }\n\n    componentDidCatch(error: Error, errorInfo: ErrorInfo): void {\n        this.setState({ errorInfo })\n\n        // Log to console with helpful formatting\n        console.error(\"[OneJS React] Error caught by ErrorBoundary:\")\n        console.error(\"  Error:\", error.message)\n        if (error.stack) {\n            console.error(\"  Stack:\", error.stack)\n        }\n        if (errorInfo.componentStack) {\n            console.error(\"  Component Stack:\", errorInfo.componentStack)\n        }\n\n        // Call user-provided error handler\n        this.props.onError?.(error, errorInfo)\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Reset the error boundary to try rendering children again.\n     * Useful after the underlying issue has been fixed.\n     */\n    reset = (): void => {\n        this.setState({\n            hasError: false,\n            error: null,\n            errorInfo: null,\n        })\n        this.props.onReset?.()\n    }\n\n    render(): ReactNode {\n        if (this.state.hasError) {\n            const { fallback } = this.props\n            const { error, errorInfo } = this.state\n\n            // Render custom fallback if provided\n            if (fallback !== undefined) {\n                if (typeof fallback === \"function\") {\n                    return fallback(error!, errorInfo!)\n                }\n                return fallback\n            }\n\n            // Default fallback UI\n            return (\n                <View style={{\n                    padding: 16,\n                    backgroundColor: \"#2d1b1b\",\n                    borderColor: \"#ff4444\",\n                    borderWidth: 2,\n                }}>\n                    <Label style={{\n                        color: \"#ff6666\",\n                        fontSize: 16,\n                        marginBottom: 8,\n                    }}>\n                        Something went wrong\n                    </Label>\n                    <Label style={{\n                        color: \"#ffaaaa\",\n                        fontSize: 12,\n                    }}>\n                        {error?.message || \"Unknown error\"}\n                    </Label>\n                </View>\n            )\n        }\n\n        return this.props.children\n    }\n}\n\n/**\n * Helper hook-style error info for functional components.\n * Note: This is a simple utility. For actual error catching,\n * you must use the ErrorBoundary class component.\n */\nexport function formatError(error: Error, componentStack?: string): string {\n    const parts = [`Error: ${error.message}`]\n\n    if (error.stack) {\n        // Get first few lines of stack\n        const stackLines = error.stack.split(\"\\n\").slice(0, 5)\n        parts.push(`Stack:\\n${stackLines.join(\"\\n\")}`)\n    }\n\n    if (componentStack) {\n        // Get first few lines of component stack\n        const compLines = componentStack.split(\"\\n\").slice(0, 5)\n        parts.push(`Component:\\n${compLines.join(\"\\n\")}`)\n    }\n\n    return parts.join(\"\\n\\n\")\n}\n", "/**\n * Responsive design system for OneJS\n *\n * Provides React context and hooks for responsive breakpoints.\n * Uses event-driven updates via GeometryChangedEvent (not polling).\n *\n * Mobile-first: At 1400px width, sm/md/lg/xl are all active (not just xl).\n */\n\nimport { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect, type ReactNode } from \"react\"\n\n// Globals from QuickJS environment\ndeclare const __root: {\n    __csHandle: number\n    resolvedStyle: {\n        width: number\n        height: number\n    }\n    AddToClassList: (className: string) => void\n    RemoveFromClassList: (className: string) => void\n}\n\ndeclare const __eventAPI: {\n    addEventListener: (element: unknown, eventType: string, callback: Function) => void\n    removeEventListener: (element: unknown, eventType: string, callback: Function) => void\n}\n\n// Breakpoint definitions (Tailwind v3 defaults)\nexport const BREAKPOINTS = {\n    sm: 640,\n    md: 768,\n    lg: 1024,\n    xl: 1280,\n    \"2xl\": 1536,\n} as const\n\nexport type BreakpointName = keyof typeof BREAKPOINTS | \"base\"\n\nexport interface ScreenContextValue {\n    /** Current viewport width in pixels */\n    width: number\n    /** Current viewport height in pixels */\n    height: number\n    /** Current breakpoint name (highest matching) */\n    breakpoint: BreakpointName\n    /** True if viewport >= 640px */\n    isSm: boolean\n    /** True if viewport >= 768px */\n    isMd: boolean\n    /** True if viewport >= 1024px */\n    isLg: boolean\n    /** True if viewport >= 1280px */\n    isXl: boolean\n    /** True if viewport >= 1536px */\n    is2xl: boolean\n}\n\nconst ScreenContext = createContext<ScreenContextValue | null>(null)\n\n/**\n * Calculate breakpoint state from viewport width\n */\nfunction calculateBreakpoints(width: number, height: number): ScreenContextValue {\n    const isSm = width >= BREAKPOINTS.sm\n    const isMd = width >= BREAKPOINTS.md\n    const isLg = width >= BREAKPOINTS.lg\n    const isXl = width >= BREAKPOINTS.xl\n    const is2xl = width >= BREAKPOINTS[\"2xl\"]\n\n    // Determine current breakpoint (highest matching)\n    let breakpoint: BreakpointName = \"base\"\n    if (is2xl) breakpoint = \"2xl\"\n    else if (isXl) breakpoint = \"xl\"\n    else if (isLg) breakpoint = \"lg\"\n    else if (isMd) breakpoint = \"md\"\n    else if (isSm) breakpoint = \"sm\"\n\n    return { width, height, breakpoint, isSm, isMd, isLg, isXl, is2xl }\n}\n\n/**\n * Apply breakpoint classes to root element (mobile-first cascading)\n */\nfunction applyBreakpointClasses(screen: ScreenContextValue) {\n    // Remove all breakpoint classes first\n    __root.RemoveFromClassList(\"sm\")\n    __root.RemoveFromClassList(\"md\")\n    __root.RemoveFromClassList(\"lg\")\n    __root.RemoveFromClassList(\"xl\")\n    __root.RemoveFromClassList(\"2xl\")\n\n    // Mobile-first: apply ALL matching breakpoints, not just highest\n    if (screen.isSm) __root.AddToClassList(\"sm\")\n    if (screen.isMd) __root.AddToClassList(\"md\")\n    if (screen.isLg) __root.AddToClassList(\"lg\")\n    if (screen.isXl) __root.AddToClassList(\"xl\")\n    if (screen.is2xl) __root.AddToClassList(\"2xl\")\n}\n\nexport interface ScreenProviderProps {\n    children: ReactNode\n    /** Custom breakpoints (optional) */\n    breakpoints?: Partial<typeof BREAKPOINTS>\n}\n\n/**\n * Provider component for responsive screen context.\n *\n * Wrap your app with this to enable responsive hooks.\n *\n * @example\n * ```tsx\n * render(\n *   <ScreenProvider>\n *     <App />\n *   </ScreenProvider>,\n *   __root\n * )\n * ```\n */\nexport function ScreenProvider({ children }: ScreenProviderProps) {\n    // Initialize with current viewport size\n    const [screen, setScreen] = useState<ScreenContextValue>(() => {\n        const width = __root.resolvedStyle?.width || 0\n        const height = __root.resolvedStyle?.height || 0\n        return calculateBreakpoints(width, height)\n    })\n\n    useEffect(() => {\n        // Apply initial breakpoint classes\n        applyBreakpointClasses(screen)\n\n        // Handle viewport change events from C#\n        const handleViewportChange = (evt: { width: number; height: number }) => {\n            const newScreen = calculateBreakpoints(evt.width, evt.height)\n            setScreen(newScreen)\n            applyBreakpointClasses(newScreen)\n        }\n\n        // Listen for viewport changes on root element\n        __eventAPI.addEventListener(__root, \"viewportchange\", handleViewportChange)\n\n        return () => {\n            __eventAPI.removeEventListener(__root, \"viewportchange\", handleViewportChange)\n        }\n    }, [])\n\n    return (\n        <ScreenContext.Provider value={screen}>\n            {children}\n        </ScreenContext.Provider>\n    )\n}\n\n/**\n * Hook to get the current breakpoint name.\n *\n * @example\n * ```tsx\n * function Component() {\n *   const breakpoint = useBreakpoint()\n *   return <Label text={`Current: ${breakpoint}`} />\n * }\n * ```\n */\nexport function useBreakpoint(): BreakpointName {\n    const ctx = useContext(ScreenContext)\n    if (!ctx) {\n        throw new Error(\"useBreakpoint must be used within ScreenProvider\")\n    }\n    return ctx.breakpoint\n}\n\n/**\n * Hook to get the current viewport size.\n *\n * @example\n * ```tsx\n * function Component() {\n *   const { width, height } = useScreenSize()\n *   return <Label text={`${width}x${height}`} />\n * }\n * ```\n */\nexport function useScreenSize(): { width: number; height: number } {\n    const ctx = useContext(ScreenContext)\n    if (!ctx) {\n        throw new Error(\"useScreenSize must be used within ScreenProvider\")\n    }\n    return { width: ctx.width, height: ctx.height }\n}\n\n/**\n * Hook to get all responsive state.\n *\n * @example\n * ```tsx\n * function Component() {\n *   const { isMd, isLg, breakpoint } = useResponsive()\n *   return (\n *     <View>\n *       {isLg && <Sidebar />}\n *       <Content />\n *     </View>\n *   )\n * }\n * ```\n */\nexport function useResponsive(): ScreenContextValue {\n    const ctx = useContext(ScreenContext)\n    if (!ctx) {\n        throw new Error(\"useResponsive must be used within ScreenProvider\")\n    }\n    return ctx\n}\n\n/**\n * Hook to check if a specific breakpoint is active.\n *\n * @example\n * ```tsx\n * function Component() {\n *   const isDesktop = useMediaQuery(\"lg\")\n *   return isDesktop ? <DesktopLayout /> : <MobileLayout />\n * }\n * ```\n */\nexport function useMediaQuery(breakpoint: keyof typeof BREAKPOINTS): boolean {\n    const ctx = useContext(ScreenContext)\n    if (!ctx) {\n        throw new Error(\"useMediaQuery must be used within ScreenProvider\")\n    }\n\n    switch (breakpoint) {\n        case \"sm\": return ctx.isSm\n        case \"md\": return ctx.isMd\n        case \"lg\": return ctx.isLg\n        case \"xl\": return ctx.isXl\n        case \"2xl\": return ctx.is2xl\n        default: return false\n    }\n}\n", "/**\n * Vector drawing utilities for OneJS.\n *\n * Provides Transform2D for applying 2D transformations to drawing coordinates,\n * since Unity's Painter2D doesn't have built-in transform support.\n *\n * Also provides useVectorContent hook for automatic repaint on dependency changes.\n */\n\nimport { useRef, useEffect, useCallback, type DependencyList, type RefObject } from 'react'\nimport type { Vector2, VisualElement, MeshGenerationContext, GenerateVisualContentCallback } from './types'\n\n// Global declarations for Unity interop\ndeclare const CS: {\n    UnityEngine: {\n        Vector2: new (x: number, y: number) => Vector2;\n    };\n};\n\n/**\n * 2D transformation helper for vector drawing.\n *\n * Unity's Painter2D doesn't support transforms (translate, rotate, scale).\n * This class provides client-side matrix math to transform coordinates\n * before passing them to Painter2D methods.\n *\n * @example\n * import { Transform2D } from \"onejs-react\"\n *\n * <View\n *     style={{ width: 200, height: 200 }}\n *     onGenerateVisualContent={(mgc) => {\n *         const p = mgc.painter2D\n *         const t = new Transform2D()\n *\n *         // Center and rotate 45 degrees\n *         t.translate(100, 100)\n *         t.rotate(Math.PI / 4)\n *\n *         // Draw a square using transformed coordinates\n *         p.BeginPath()\n *         p.MoveTo(t.point(-40, -40))\n *         p.LineTo(t.point(40, -40))\n *         p.LineTo(t.point(40, 40))\n *         p.LineTo(t.point(-40, 40))\n *         p.ClosePath()\n *         p.Fill()\n *     }}\n * />\n */\nexport class Transform2D {\n    // Current transformation matrix (a, b, c, d, e, f)\n    // [ a  c  e ]   [ x ]   [ a*x + c*y + e ]\n    // [ b  d  f ] * [ y ] = [ b*x + d*y + f ]\n    // [ 0  0  1 ]   [ 1 ]   [       1       ]\n    private _a: number = 1\n    private _b: number = 0\n    private _c: number = 0\n    private _d: number = 1\n    private _e: number = 0\n    private _f: number = 0\n\n    // State stack for save/restore\n    private _stack: Array<[number, number, number, number, number, number]> = []\n\n    /**\n     * Save the current transformation state to the stack.\n     * Use restore() to return to this state later.\n     */\n    save(): void {\n        this._stack.push([this._a, this._b, this._c, this._d, this._e, this._f])\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Restore the most recently saved transformation state.\n     * If the stack is empty, resets to identity.\n     */\n    restore(): void {\n        const state = this._stack.pop()\n        if (state) {\n            [this._a, this._b, this._c, this._d, this._e, this._f] = state\n        } else {\n            this.reset()\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Reset the transformation to identity (no transformation).\n     */\n    reset(): void {\n        this._a = 1\n        this._b = 0\n        this._c = 0\n        this._d = 1\n        this._e = 0\n        this._f = 0\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Apply a translation (move the origin).\n     * @param x - Horizontal translation\n     * @param y - Vertical translation\n     */\n    translate(x: number, y: number): void {\n        // new_e = a*x + c*y + e\n        // new_f = b*x + d*y + f\n        this._e += this._a * x + this._c * y\n        this._f += this._b * x + this._d * y\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Apply a rotation around the current origin.\n     * @param angle - Rotation angle in radians (clockwise)\n     */\n    rotate(angle: number): void {\n        const cos = Math.cos(angle)\n        const sin = Math.sin(angle)\n\n        // Multiply current matrix by rotation matrix\n        const a = this._a * cos + this._c * sin\n        const b = this._b * cos + this._d * sin\n        const c = this._a * -sin + this._c * cos\n        const d = this._b * -sin + this._d * cos\n\n        this._a = a\n        this._b = b\n        this._c = c\n        this._d = d\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Apply a scale transformation.\n     * @param x - Horizontal scale factor\n     * @param y - Vertical scale factor (defaults to x for uniform scale)\n     */\n    scale(x: number, y?: number): void {\n        const sy = y ?? x\n\n        this._a *= x\n        this._b *= x\n        this._c *= sy\n        this._d *= sy\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Transform a point using the current transformation matrix.\n     * @param x - X coordinate in local space\n     * @param y - Y coordinate in local space\n     * @returns Transformed point as Unity Vector2\n     */\n    point(x: number, y: number): Vector2 {\n        const tx = this._a * x + this._c * y + this._e\n        const ty = this._b * x + this._d * y + this._f\n        return new CS.UnityEngine.Vector2(tx, ty)\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Transform multiple points at once.\n     * @param coords - Array of [x, y] coordinate pairs\n     * @returns Array of transformed Vector2 points\n     *\n     * @example\n     * const corners = t.points([-40, -40], [40, -40], [40, 40], [-40, 40])\n     * p.MoveTo(corners[0])\n     * for (let i = 1; i < corners.length; i++) p.LineTo(corners[i])\n     */\n    points(...coords: [number, number][]): Vector2[] {\n        return coords.map(([x, y]) => this.point(x, y))\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Get the raw transformation values.\n     * Useful for debugging or advanced matrix operations.\n     *\n     * Returns [a, b, c, d, e, f] where:\n     * - a, d: scale\n     * - b, c: rotation/skew\n     * - e, f: translation\n     */\n    get values(): [number, number, number, number, number, number] {\n        return [this._a, this._b, this._c, this._d, this._e, this._f]\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Set the transformation matrix directly.\n     * @param a - Horizontal scale (1 = no scale)\n     * @param b - Vertical skew\n     * @param c - Horizontal skew\n     * @param d - Vertical scale (1 = no scale)\n     * @param e - Horizontal translation\n     * @param f - Vertical translation\n     */\n    setTransform(a: number, b: number, c: number, d: number, e: number, f: number): void {\n        this._a = a\n        this._b = b\n        this._c = c\n        this._d = d\n        this._e = e\n        this._f = f\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Multiply the current matrix by another matrix.\n     * @param a - Horizontal scale\n     * @param b - Vertical skew\n     * @param c - Horizontal skew\n     * @param d - Vertical scale\n     * @param e - Horizontal translation\n     * @param f - Vertical translation\n     */\n    transform(a: number, b: number, c: number, d: number, e: number, f: number): void {\n        const a_ = this._a * a + this._c * b\n        const b_ = this._b * a + this._d * b\n        const c_ = this._a * c + this._c * d\n        const d_ = this._b * c + this._d * d\n        const e_ = this._a * e + this._c * f + this._e\n        const f_ = this._b * e + this._d * f + this._f\n\n        this._a = a_\n        this._b = b_\n        this._c = c_\n        this._d = d_\n        this._e = e_\n        this._f = f_\n    }\n}\n\n/**\n * Hook for vector drawing with automatic repaint on dependency changes.\n *\n * Returns a ref to attach to a VisualElement. When dependencies change,\n * automatically calls MarkDirtyRepaint() to trigger a redraw.\n *\n * @param draw - Drawing callback that receives MeshGenerationContext\n * @param deps - Dependency array (like useEffect) - repaint when these change\n * @returns Ref to attach to the element\n *\n * @example\n * ```tsx\n * function AnimatedCircle() {\n *     const [radius, setRadius] = useState(50)\n *\n *     const ref = useVectorContent((mgc) => {\n *         const p = mgc.painter2D\n *         const Angle = CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.Angle\n *\n *         p.fillColor = new CS.UnityEngine.Color(1, 0, 0, 1)\n *         p.BeginPath()\n *         p.Arc(\n *             new CS.UnityEngine.Vector2(100, 100),\n *             radius,\n *             Angle.Degrees(0),\n *             Angle.Degrees(360),\n *             CS.UnityEngine.UIElements.ArcDirection.Clockwise\n *         )\n *         p.Fill()\n *     }, [radius]) // Auto-repaints when radius changes\n *\n *     return <View ref={ref} style={{ width: 200, height: 200 }} />\n * }\n * ```\n */\nexport function useVectorContent(\n    draw: GenerateVisualContentCallback,\n    deps: DependencyList = []\n): RefObject<VisualElement | null> {\n    const ref = useRef<VisualElement | null>(null)\n    const drawRef = useRef(draw)\n\n    // Keep drawRef current\n    drawRef.current = draw\n\n    // Register callback and handle updates\n    useEffect(() => {\n        const element = ref.current\n        if (!element) return\n\n        // Create a stable wrapper that always calls the latest draw function\n        const callback: GenerateVisualContentCallback = (mgc) => {\n            drawRef.current(mgc)\n        }\n\n        // Assign the callback to generateVisualContent\n        // Use unknown cast because VisualElement interface doesn't expose this property directly\n        const el = element as unknown as { generateVisualContent: GenerateVisualContentCallback | null }\n        el.generateVisualContent = callback\n\n        // Initial repaint to render content\n        element.MarkDirtyRepaint()\n\n        return () => {\n            // Clear callback on cleanup\n            el.generateVisualContent = null\n        }\n    }, []) // Only run once on mount\n\n    // Trigger repaint when dependencies change (but not on first render)\n    const isFirstRender = useRef(true)\n    useEffect(() => {\n        if (isFirstRender.current) {\n            isFirstRender.current = false\n            return\n        }\n\n        const element = ref.current\n        if (element) {\n            element.MarkDirtyRepaint()\n        }\n    }, deps)\n\n    return ref\n}\n", "import { useState, useEffect, useRef, useReducer } from \"react\"\n\n// QuickJS environment declarations\ndeclare function requestAnimationFrame(callback: (time: number) => void): number;\ndeclare function cancelAnimationFrame(id: number): void;\n\n/**\n * Syncs a value from C# (or any external source) to React state, checking every frame.\n *\n * Has two modes depending on whether a `select` function is provided:\n *\n * **Simple mode** (no selector): Compares values with `Object.is`. Best for primitives\n * (numbers, strings, booleans) and cases where the getter returns a new object each time.\n *\n * **Selector mode**: Extracts an array of comparable values to watch. Re-renders only when\n * any selected value changes. Essential for C# proxy objects where the proxy reference is\n * cached \u2014 without a selector, you'd be comparing the same proxy to itself.\n * The returned value is always read fresh from the getter during render.\n *\n * @param getter - Function that returns the current value (called every frame)\n * @param selectOrDeps - Either a selector function or a dependency array\n * @param deps - Optional dependency array (only when using a selector)\n * @returns The current value, updated each frame if changed\n *\n * @example\n * // Simple: sync a C# property (primitives)\n * const health = useFrameSync(() => player.Health)\n * const score = useFrameSync(() => gameManager.Score)\n *\n * @example\n * // Selector: watch specific properties on a C# proxy object\n * const place = useFrameSync(\n *     () => gameState.currentPlace,\n *     (p) => [p?.Name, p?.NPCs?.Count, p?.Items?.Count]\n * )\n *\n * @example\n * // Selector: with a version stamp from C#\n * const quest = useFrameSync(\n *     () => questManager.activeQuest ?? null,\n *     (q) => [q?.Version]\n * )\n *\n * @example\n * // Simple with dependencies (if the source reference can change)\n * const health = useFrameSync(() => currentPlayer.Health, [currentPlayer])\n */\nexport function useFrameSync<T>(\n    getter: () => T,\n    selectOrDeps?: ((value: T) => readonly unknown[]) | readonly unknown[],\n    deps?: readonly unknown[]\n): T {\n    // Determine which mode we're in\n    const hasSelector = typeof selectOrDeps === \"function\"\n    const select = hasSelector ? selectOrDeps as (value: T) => readonly unknown[] : undefined\n    const effectDeps = hasSelector ? (deps ?? []) : (selectOrDeps as readonly unknown[] ?? [])\n\n    if (select) {\n        return useFrameSyncSelect(getter, select, effectDeps)\n    } else {\n        return useFrameSyncSimple(getter, effectDeps)\n    }\n}\n\n/** Simple mode: compare with Object.is. */\nfunction useFrameSyncSimple<T>(getter: () => T, deps: readonly unknown[]): T {\n    const getInitialValue = (): T => {\n        try {\n            return getter()\n        } catch {\n            return undefined as T\n        }\n    }\n\n    const [value, setValue] = useState<T>(getInitialValue)\n    const lastValueRef = useRef<T>(value)\n    const getterRef = useRef(getter)\n    const runningRef = useRef(false)\n\n    getterRef.current = getter\n\n    useEffect(() => {\n        try {\n            const initial = getterRef.current()\n            lastValueRef.current = initial\n            setValue(initial)\n        } catch {\n            // Getter failed, keep current value\n        }\n\n        runningRef.current = true\n\n        const check = () => {\n            if (!runningRef.current) return\n\n            try {\n                const current = getterRef.current()\n                if (!Object.is(current, lastValueRef.current)) {\n                    lastValueRef.current = current\n                    setValue(current)\n                }\n            } catch {\n                // Getter might fail if object was destroyed - that's ok\n            }\n\n            if (runningRef.current) {\n                requestAnimationFrame(check)\n            }\n        }\n\n        requestAnimationFrame(check)\n\n        return () => {\n            runningRef.current = false\n        }\n    }, deps)\n\n    return value\n}\n\n/** Selector mode: extract comparable values, always return fresh from getter. */\nfunction useFrameSyncSelect<T>(\n    getter: () => T,\n    select: (value: T) => readonly unknown[],\n    deps: readonly unknown[]\n): T {\n    const [, forceRender] = useReducer((x: number) => x + 1, 0)\n    const getterRef = useRef(getter)\n    const selectRef = useRef(select)\n    const lastSelectedRef = useRef<readonly unknown[]>([])\n    const runningRef = useRef(false)\n    const initializedRef = useRef(false)\n\n    getterRef.current = getter\n    selectRef.current = select\n\n    // Initialize dependency tracking on first render\n    if (!initializedRef.current) {\n        initializedRef.current = true\n        try {\n            const val = getter()\n            lastSelectedRef.current = select(val)\n        } catch {\n            // Getter or select failed, keep empty deps\n        }\n    }\n\n    useEffect(() => {\n        try {\n            const val = getterRef.current()\n            lastSelectedRef.current = selectRef.current(val)\n        } catch {\n            // Getter or select failed\n        }\n\n        runningRef.current = true\n\n        const check = () => {\n            if (!runningRef.current) return\n\n            try {\n                const current = getterRef.current()\n                const selected = selectRef.current(current)\n                const prev = lastSelectedRef.current\n                const changed = selected.length !== prev.length ||\n                    selected.some((val, i) => !Object.is(val, prev[i]))\n\n                if (changed) {\n                    lastSelectedRef.current = selected\n                    forceRender()\n                }\n            } catch {\n                // Getter or select might fail if object was destroyed\n            }\n\n            if (runningRef.current) {\n                requestAnimationFrame(check)\n            }\n        }\n\n        requestAnimationFrame(check)\n\n        return () => {\n            runningRef.current = false\n        }\n    }, deps)\n\n    // Always read fresh from getter during render.\n    // This ensures we return the latest proxy with current C# state.\n    try {\n        return getterRef.current()\n    } catch {\n        return undefined as T\n    }\n}\n\n/**\n * @deprecated Use `useFrameSync` with a selector instead.\n *\n * `useFrameSyncWith` compares the value returned by the getter using a custom\n * equality function. However, this does NOT work with C# proxy objects because\n * the proxy reference is cached \u2014 you end up comparing the same object to itself.\n *\n * Instead, use `useFrameSync` with a selector that extracts comparable values:\n * ```ts\n * // Before (broken with C# proxies):\n * const pos = useFrameSyncWith(\n *     () => transform.position,\n *     (a, b) => a.x === b.x && a.y === b.y &&
